title: From fridge yetis to real results: how the HMRC app is helping people take control of their money and tax
authors: Alice McCallum-Sealy, Senior Communication Manager, HMRC

content.text: HMRC’s Transformation Roadmap sets out a clear ambition: to make everyday tax interactions simpler and more intuitive to meet customer needs. The increased traffic to the HMRC app this year reflects HMRC’s longer‑term ambition to make it easier for customers to self‑serve on straightforward tasks.

Decisions about what to prioritise, how to communicate and where to intervene are shaped by service data, user research and behavioural insight. This allows us to identify where customers commonly experience friction, delay or uncertainty, and to design targeted prompts, messages and content that encourage earlier, simpler action. By aligning communications with real customer behaviours and moments of need, we’ve been able to drive meaningful uptake and sustained engagement with the HMRC app as part of everyday tax interactions.
This data led approach to behaviour change has supported sustained adoption of the app, which has encouraged:
From parents to pensioners, millions of people across the UK are increasingly using the app to stay in control of their money and tax. Record numbers are regularly using it to:

HMRC has embedded the app into the wider service ecosystem, supported by a strong, insight-led communications approach. The creative approach has been designed to stand out against a busy media landscape and resonate with our customers in a fun and distinctive way. Whether it's the surreal charm of a bathtub limo and rubber duck serenade, or the offbeat oddity of a yeti in the fridge, we’ve captured that feeling of being in control – even when life gets chaotic.
Take a look at the HMRC adverts here:
You’re on it 2.0 – Download the HMRC app
You’re on it – Download the HMRC app

The increase up-take of self-serve interactions has reduced telephony contact, supported departmental efficiency savings and has allowed HMRC to focus more telephony resource on complex cases and customers who need additional support.

For teams across government, HMRC’s experience shows how digital transformation is not just about building new tools, but about using insight to shape how we communicate changes and how services are experienced at scale, ensuring they fit naturally into people’s lives.

Download and explore the HMRC app to quickly and easily access a wide range of services, with guidance available on GOV.UK. Recommend the app to family and friends to help more people self-serve online and free up advisers for those who need extra support.
